introSection: Bergbanan was a wooden roller coaster located at Liseberg in Gothenburg, Västra Götaland, Sweden. It was manufactured by Waldemar Lebech. It opened in 1923 and closed in 1987.

sections.title: History
sections.content: Bergbanan was Liseberg’s first roller coaster and opened in the same year as the park, in 1923. When it opened, it was the longest roller coaster in the world. It closed in 1987 and was demolished.

sections.title: Båtbannan
sections.content: From 1955 to 1972, there was a water dark ride in Bergbanan called Båtbanan. Similar to how it is at Tivoli Gardens with Rutschebanen and Minen.

fullText:
Bergbanan was a wooden roller coaster located at Liseberg in Gothenburg, Västra Götaland, Sweden. It was manufactured by Waldemar Lebech. It opened in 1923 and closed in 1987.

Bergbanan was Liseberg’s first roller coaster and opened in the same year as the park, in 1923. When it opened, it was the longest roller coaster in the world. It closed in 1987 and was demolished.

From 1955 to 1972, there was a water dark ride in Bergbanan called Båtbanan. Similar to how it is at Tivoli Gardens with Rutschebanen and Minen.
